name: Spanakopites Apo Ti Samos (Spinach Pies from Samos)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------TO FINISH----------------------------------
10 Filo pastry sheets
1/2 cup Olive oil
OVEN TEMPERATURE: 190°C (375°F); COOKING TIME: 40 Minutes
1. Wash spinach well and remove any coarse stems. Chop coarsely.
2. Put into a large pan, cover, and place over heat for 7-8 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally or turning with a fork.
3. Heat just long enough to wilt spinach so that juices can run freely. Drain well in a colander, pressing occasionally with a spoon.
- If using frozen spinach, defrost, drain well, and chop.
4. Place in a mixing bowl.
5. Heat oil in a frying pan and gently fry spring onions until soft. Add the oil and onion to the spinach mixture with cheese, herbs, eggs, and nutmeg. Mix well to combine, then add salt and pepper to taste.
6. Unfold filo pastry sheets and cut into halves approximately 20 x 30 cm (8 x 12 inches) in size.
7. Stack and cover with a cloth. Take a sheet and brush lightly with oil. Fold in half to make a strip about 10 cm (4 inches) wide.
8. Brush again with oil and spread about 2 tablespoons of filling across one long edge, leaving the sides clear of filling. Fold in the sides, then roll up firmly.
9. Bend the roll into a coil and place in an oiled baking dish. Repeat with remaining ingredients, placing them in one layer in the baking dish.
10. Brush tops lightly with oil and bake in a moderately hot oven for 25 minutes until golden brown. Serve hot or cold as an appetizer or light meal. If serving cold, use butter instead of olive oil unless your oil is of high quality.
